reasoning: pH has drifted into the 5.6–5.7 range over the past 24 hours (24hr_change: −0.14), but this is still above the hard danger line of 6.0. The 20% water change on May 5th would normally raise pH; the current reading suggests either the tank's carbonate buffering is genuinely weak or the probe displacement on May 4th corrupted baseline data. A cautious re-baseline water test would clarify the true trend before the cycle completes.
